_provenance.stage: Stage 6 — Synthesis (retroactive rebuild from Codex Stage 3)
version_history: v3 (2026-05-17): retroactive editorial pass. Stage 3 (Codex) FAS=72 with 5 verified claims. Hook 'Maternal citizenship is now in the Constitution' (INCORRECT — amendment passed but automatic provision only operates from commencement) replaced with 'Parliament passed the maternal-citizenship amendment in 2024'. Pre-amendment scope tightened to the defined class (under 18, born overseas to Malaysian mothers + foreign fathers). Residual-gaps card sharpened to specifics: non-retroactivity, pre-implementation births, adults over 18, stateless local-born outside settlement class. View card 'closes the central injustice' softened to 'substantially addresses' (Stage 3 OVERCLAIM flag).

revision_log: CORRECTED (Stage 3): INCORRECT — Hook 'Maternal citizenship is now in the Constitution' → 'Parliament passed the maternal-citizenship amendment in 2024' (the amendment was passed but automatic provision operates from commencement, not on a flag-day basis).

CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'Pre-2024 children needed a separate Federal Court settlement' → 'Pre-amendment children needed a separate Federal Court settlement' (more accurate temporal framing); also class clarified (under 18, overseas-born, Malaysian mother + foreign father).
REPHRASED (Stage 3): Fact 1 sub now specifies the amendment language change (father-only → parent-inclusive) and notes 'operates from commencement, not retroactively' per Stage 3.

REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'Dewan Negara followed on 3 December 2024' softened to 'reporting and party statements place the Dewan Negara vote on 3 December 2024' (Stage 3 did not locate official Hansard).
REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'a five-member Federal Court bench endorsed' softened to 'a Federal Court bench endorsed' (specific bench size not in primary source).

CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Card 4 'gaps including some categories of stateless children, foundling-status disputes, and adult children' replaced with specific residual gaps (non-retroactivity, pre-implementation births, adults over 18, stateless local-born outside settlement class).
CORRECTED (Stage 3): OVERCLAIM — View 'closes the central injustice' softened to 'substantially addresses the central injustice'.
PRESERVED (Stage 3 VERIFIED): 17 October 2024 Dewan Rakyat vote; Suriani Kempe v Kerajaan Malaysia case timeline; Article 15(2) pathway for the settlement class; Family Frontiers continued advocacy.
